sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
sources.population: Adults receiving cardiovascular risk assessment or lipid-modification review for primary or secondary prevention.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary care and other clinician-led cardiovascular prevention services.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult cardiovascular risk and lipid-modification assessment and monitoring; international only and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.12 — cardiovascular risk and lifestyle review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.5.7 — lipid assessment and baseline tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports full lipid profile, blood pressure, BMI, diabetes status, renal function, liver transaminases, thyroid context, muscle symptoms, and clinician-led specialist assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.11.12 — treatment response and annual review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented adherence, adverse-effect review, lifestyle and cardiovascular risk discussion, repeat lipid and liver testing, annual medication review, and agreed follow-up without mapping doses.

archive.title: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.population: Adults receiving cardiovascular risk assessment or lipid-modification review for primary or secondary prevention.
archive.setting: Primary care and other clinician-led cardiovascular prevention services.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.12 — cardiovascular risk and lifestyle review
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.5.7 — lipid assessment and baseline tests
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports full lipid profile, blood pressure, BMI, diabetes status, renal function, liver transaminases, thyroid context, muscle symptoms, and clinician-led specialist assessment.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.11.12 — treatment response and annual review
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented adherence, adverse-effect review, lifestyle and cardiovascular risk discussion, repeat lipid and liver testing, annual medication review, and agreed follow-up without mapping doses.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.version: NICE NG23; last updated and reviewed 2026-04-15
sources.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people registered female at birth with menopause-associated symptoms.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for menopause symptom identification, selected test limitations, shared discussion, and review; international and partial for UAE prescribing and local referral practice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 — information and support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists menstrual-cycle changes, vasomotor, genitourinary, mood, musculoskeletal, and sexual symptoms and their variable severity and duration.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — identifying perimenopause and menopause
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines symptom-and-cycle-based identification in people aged 45 or over and limits laboratory and imaging tests, including FSH in specified contexts.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.3 — discussing management options
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires benefit-risk discussion tailored to age, circumstances, risk factors, preferences, and repeated review when HRT is chosen.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.5–1.5.13 — genitourinary symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Covers clinician-discussed moisturiser, lubricant, and vaginal-treatment options and ongoing review for genitourinary symptoms.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.9.3 — reviewing treatment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines documented review at three months, annually thereafter, or earlier for ineffectiveness, adverse effects, or clinical need.
